_meta.purpose: Resolve findings[].category enum + favicon_hash field location. Unblocks BLUE-AUTH-007, BLUE-EXP-004, tightens RED-AUTH-001.
_meta.method: SOURCE-authoritative (detector/category definitions) + one localhost-only empirical run for schema confirmation
_meta.target: disposable local Flowise 1.6.5 (flowiseai/flowise:1.6.5), loopback capture, torn down + image removed after run

favicon_hash.verdict: ABSENT — no struct field named FaviconHash / json tag favicon_hash anywhere in aimap
favicon_hash.source_evidence: grep favicon_hash|FaviconHash across ~/ai-recon/aimap/*.go = 0 struct fields. The only 'favicon' hits are body_contains fingerprint probes (favicon-32x32.png, /promptfoo/favicon) and a Shodan-dork comment ('favicon hash 611531125' for Kubecost) — none are emitted output fields.
favicon_hash.empirical_evidence: Live aimap JSON (below) carries favicon.ico in body_snippet yet emits NO favicon_hash at any level (open_ports[], services[], enum_results[], summary{}).
favicon_hash.impact: RED-RECON-003 / BLUE-EXP-004 favicon survey_signal is NOT wirable to aimap as written. Favicon-hash matching lives in Shodan dorks (http.favicon.hash:), not aimap output. Either (a) re-point the check to a Shodan/Censys favicon facet, or (b) add a FaviconHash field to aimap's PortResult (mmh3 of base64 favicon) as a new-tool task. Until then BLUE-EXP-004 stays stubbed.

findings_category_enum.note: Authoritative list extracted from aimap source (Finding.Category string literals). 46 base (v1.9.39) + 6 added v1.9.40-1.9.46 = 52 total.

exec_buildflow_access._meaning: (a) unauthenticated code-exec / flow-build / access — maps to BLUE-AUTH-001, RED-AUTH-001, RED-INJ flow surfaces
exec_buildflow_access.rce: Critical — unauth remote code execution, no exploit needed (Jupyter, Metabase CVE-2023-38646, n8n)
exec_buildflow_access.rce_by_design: Critical — RCE is intended behavior (n8n custom-node install)
exec_buildflow_access.unauth_api: Critical — full API reachable without auth (ComfyUI, A1111, InvokeAI)
exec_buildflow_access.unauthenticated-access: unauthenticated access confirmed (MCP enumerator variant, v1.9.45)
exec_buildflow_access.flows: Critical — workflow/flow definitions readable (n8n, Flowise, Langflow)

token_credential_return._meaning: (b) live-token / credential return — maps to BLUE-AUTH-007, RED-AUTH-003
token_credential_return.exfil_credential: Critical — LIVE credential/token returned in HTTP response. THIS is the BLUE-AUTH-007 / RED-AUTH-003 anchor. Emitted by scanCredentials/scanSecrets in enum_credentials.go when a vendor key pattern (langfuse, openai, etc.) is found in a response body; value is redacted via redactKey() before the finding is written.
token_credential_return.credentials: Critical — credential store/endpoint accessible, batch exposure (Flowise /api/v1/credentials, n8n /rest/credentials, Superset DB conns)
token_credential_return.default_credentials: Critical — vendor-default user/pass valid, access_token obtainable
token_credential_return.secrets: secret values or API keys detected in responses
token_credential_return.SPEND_LEAK: High — billing/cost/usage metrics exposed (may carry key identifiers)

other_categories._meaning: exposure/recon/info categories — not exec and not token-return
other_categories.context: service stores sensitive operational context data
other_categories.data_exposure: sensitive data exposed via unauth endpoints

captured_schema._note: Real aimap JSON from the localhost Flowise run. Target IP/port REDACTED. Demonstrates the field tree and that favicon_hash is absent at every level. findings=null here because exhaustive-scan mis-fingerprinted Flowise 1.6.5 as Argilla (the '1.6.5' version string collided with Argilla's version pattern — a known -scan-all-fingerprints artifact) so enumFlowise did not run; a fresh container also has no stored credential to surface as exfil_credential.

open_ports.body_snippet: <!DOCTYPE html><html lang="en"><head><title>Flowise - Low-code LLM apps builder</title><link rel="icon" href="favicon.ico" /> ... [favicon.ico present in HTML but NO favicon_hash field is emitted]
